active_projects.title: Enforce plan checkbox tracking during implementation and orchestration
active_projects.description: Fix plan checkbox drift during /implement and /orchestrate by adding three enforcement mechanisms: (1) Strengthen the self-review gate in general-implementation-agent.md Stage 4D-ii — make it a hard requirement that any phase marked [COMPLETED] must have all - [ ] items checked off or annotated as deviations before proceeding. (2) Add a postflight plan-checkbox validation step in skill-implementer SKILL.md — after the agent finishes, compare completed work against unchecked plan items and auto-fix mismatches via Edit. (3) Extend the orchestrator handoff schema (.orchestrator-handoff.json) to include a subtasks_completed array (e.g. ["1.1", "1.2", "2.1"]) so the orchestrator has per-subtask visibility when dispatching successors. Files to modify: .claude/agents/general-implementation-agent.md, .claude/skills/skill-implementer/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/skill-orchestrate/SKILL.md, .claude/context/patterns/orchestrator-handoff.md (if exists)

artifacts.summary: Research report with exact insertion points for all three enforcement mechanisms
3-phase plan: harden self-review gate, add postflight checkbox scan, extend handoff schema
Execution summary for plan checkbox tracking enforcement

active_projects.completion_summary: Added three plan checkbox enforcement mechanisms: hardened Stage 4D-ii self-review gate, added postflight checkbox scan in skill-implementer, extended orchestrator handoff schema with subtasks_completed

active_projects.title: Defer orchestrate commits until first implementation cycle
active_projects.description: Modify /orchestrate commit behavior: (1) In skill-orchestrate SKILL.md Stage 5, add a git commit after each implementation dispatch (status=implemented or partial with phases_completed>0), bundling all uncommitted artifacts from prior research/plan cycles. Skip commits for researched/planned statuses. (2) In orchestrate.md CHECKPOINT 3, make the final commit conditional — only commit if there are uncommitted changes (avoid empty/duplicate commits when per-implementation-cycle commits already captured everything). Files to modify: .claude/skills/skill-orchestrate/SKILL.md, .claude/commands/orchestrate.md

artifacts.summary: Full commit-flow analysis with exact insertion locations, decision table, edge cases, and recommended approach
3-phase plan: per-cycle commit in skill-orchestrate Stage 5, conditional CHECKPOINT 3, conditional multi-task batch commit
Implementation summary: deferred orchestrate commits to implementation cycles

active_projects.completion_summary: Modified /orchestrate commit behavior: per-implementation-cycle commits in skill-orchestrate Stage 5, conditional CHECKPOINT 3 and multi-task batch commits in orchestrate.md

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive analysis of Neovim and WezTerm configurations showing no automatic directory-changing behavior

active_projects.title: Investigate terminal directory change when opening neovim in wezterm
active_projects.description: Investigate why the terminal working directory changes to a project root when opening neovim sessions in wezterm from the home directory (~). Determine whether this behavior is caused by neovim or wezterm (configured in ~/.dotfiles/config/). Identify if any functionality depends on this behavior before modifying it. Goal is to avoid changing the terminal directory unless necessary.

artifacts.summary: Research report documenting OAuth2 token expiration as root cause and fix procedure
3-phase implementation plan for fixing Gmail OAuth2 authentication

active_projects.title: Fix Himalaya SMTP authentication failure when sending emails
active_projects.description: Fix Gmail SMTP authentication failure when sending emails via Himalaya (<leader>me). Error: Authentication failed: Code: 535, Enhanced code: 5.7.8, Message: Username and Password not accepted. The error occurs with TLS connection attempts and persists through multiple retry attempts. Identify and fix the root cause of the SMTP credential configuration.

active_projects.title: Standardize AI Tools Used section across PR templates and agents
active_projects.description: Fix inconsistent ## AI Tools Used section in PR description generation. Three files need changes: (1) cslib-implementation-agent.md -- replace the vague [describe what it did] placeholder with a reference to the canonical template in pr-description-format.md; (2) pr.md command -- change ## AI Disclosure heading to ## AI Tools Used in the Step 9 path/description template, and align text with the canonical format; (3) pr-description-format.md is already correct (canonical source, no changes needed). Files to modify: .claude/extensions/cslib/agents/cslib-implementation-agent.md, .claude/extensions/cslib/commands/pr.md

artifacts.summary: 3-phase plan to standardize AI Tools Used section across cslib-implementation-agent.md and pr.md

active_projects.title: Include pr-description.md in feature branch during /pr workflow
active_projects.description: Modify the /pr command to copy pr-description.md from specs/{NNN}_{SLUG}/ into the cslib repo (e.g., as pr-description.md at repo root) after STEP 9 and before STEP 10. The file should be left unstaged (not git-added) so the user can review the full PR description alongside the code changes before pushing. This gives the user a convenient way to inspect the PR description in the context of the feature branch. Files to modify: .claude/extensions/cslib/commands/pr.md
